seo.title: Oregon Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Oregon — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license oregon, dental assistant certification oregon, RDA oregon, DANB CDA oregon, dental assistant radiography oregon

license_types.title: Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Oregon.

steps.title: Complete a Board-Approved Course
steps.description: Complete a CODA accredited dental assisting program or a board-approved EFDA course.

steps.title: Pass the Oregon Expanded Functions Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Oregon Expanded Functions Examination administered by the Oregon Board of Dentistry.

steps.title: Apply for Certification
steps.description: Submit an application and pay the required fee to the Oregon Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Oregon Board of Dentistry by certification.

faqs.question: Does Oregon require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oregon requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Oregon?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Oregon.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Oregon?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Oregon?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Oregon Board of Dentistry by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Oregon
hero.dek: Oregon Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) certification requires successful completion of a board-approved course, passing the Oregon Expanded Functions Examination, and maintaining current CPR certification.
